sprint: F5 — Explicit frozen-core FCI on NaH (W1e closure attempt)
position: F4 follow-on; W1e reclassified to FCI correlation wall

step_1.diagnostic: algebraic cross-block 2-body Coulomb J - K over Na [Ne] core
step_1.rationale: 0.5 Ha < sum < 2 Ha; correction is the right SIGN (repulsive) but too small to take 4.37 Ha wall into the 0.0375-0.150 Ha closure window. Predicted residual 3.25 Ha = 43x experimental D_e.

step_2_3_4: NOT EXECUTED — Step 1 predicts predictably outside closure window; per diagnostic-before-engineering rule, stop and report.
W1e_diagnosis: Core-electron Hartree mean-field is the right SIGN but structurally insufficient magnitude. The dominant W1e correlation channels involve more than just the bonding-core Coulomb J - K coupling.
next_recommendations: Sprint F6 candidate 1: Schmidt-orthogonalize bonding orbital against [Ne] core BEFORE FCI (mathematically definitive but expensive ~3-4 weeks).

Sprint F6 candidate 2: extend the valence active space beyond max_n=2 (closer to standard quantum chemistry CASSCF/CASPT2; tests whether the W1e wall is also a basis-truncation effect at the valence level).
Sprint F6 candidate 3: pivot to next paper-writing batch — F5 diagnostic + F4 + F3 + F1+F2 give a clean documentation arc for Papers 17/19 §W1c-residual revision.
core_density_check: [Ne] core integrates to 9.9999 electrons (CR exponents Z_eff = n * zeta)
